name: understand-knowledge
tagline: Scans a Karpathy-pattern wiki directory, runs two bundled Python scripts plus LLM subagents, and builds an interactive knowledge-graph dashb
verdict: Mostly solid: explicit prompt-injection guard for article content, a guarded rm -rf that can't expand to root, clear phased steps and failure handling. Two risks for installers: the actual parsing/merge logic lives in bundled .py scripts not visible in SKILL.md (unverified in this review), and intermediate files are deleted automatically with no user confirm

highlights.summary: Phase 5 step 5 runs `rm -rf "$UA_DIR/intermediate"` automatically with no user confirmation prompt; risk is scoped (only the skill's own regenerable intermediate dir) and guarded against empty-path ro

Two executable python scripts (parse-knowledge-base.py, merge-knowledge-graph.py) are referenced as bundled but their contents were not available in this SKILL.md-only review — the actual filesystem-t

No explicit 'do not use when' guidance in the description; Phase 1 does handle a non-matching directory at runtime (script errors, tells user it isn't a Karpathy-pattern wiki), but this isn't surfaced
